tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From Prison Cell to 100-Mile Victory: A Runner's Redemption
stories.summary: Exonerated after three wrongful years in prison, Greg Kelly ran 100 miles to transform his pain into hope and reclaim his life.

stories.headline: A Stranger's Act: Dad Saves Collapsed Man with CPR, Then Explains Why
stories.summary: Jeff Gulla, 45, donated blood that morning, then performed CPR on a collapsed stranger that evening—proving service means showing up when it matters most.

stories.headline: At 91, He's Reclaiming the Appalachian Trail—Again
stories.summary: At 91, Dale Sanders is hiking the 2,190-mile Appalachian Trail to reclaim his record as oldest completer, tackling terrain twice as challenging as when he first succeeded at 82.

stories.headline: Stolen Farm Stand Returns Home, Thanks to Neighbors Who Cared
stories.summary: When thieves took a beloved pink farm stand, neighbors barely knew rallied police tips to bring back their community's gathering place within days.

stories.headline: Ranchers, Strangers, and Cooks Rally: Community Rises for Fire Evacuees
stories.summary: As wildfire forced evacuations, neighbors instantly mobilized—hauling livestock, providing hay, cooking meals, and offering shelter without being asked.

stories.headline: After 60 Years Lost, Grey Hornbills Thrive Again in Indian Forests
stories.summary: Grey Hornbills vanished from Gir forests for over 60 years; they've now bred successfully for four consecutive years, signaling a self-sustaining population's return.

stories.headline: Within 48 Hours, a Town Fills a Gymnasium With Hope for Fire Victims
stories.summary: Within 48 hours of a July 10 apartment fire, New Westminster neighbors filled Lord Kelvin gymnasium with thousands in donations and sorted clothing for displaced families.

stories.headline: From Near-Extinction to Thousands: Sea Turtles Show How Hope Endures
stories.summary: Kemp's ridley sea turtles, decimated by 99%, now inspire wonder—thousands of nests annually through decades of dedicated global conservation.

stories.headline: Robot Surgeon Makes History: First Live Operation in Operating Room
stories.summary: Surgie, a teleoperated humanoid robot controlled by an expert surgeon, successfully removed a gallbladder in an operating room, marking robots' surgical debut.

stories.headline: Florida's Rarest Bat Rebounds: Over 200 Bonneted Bats Now Thrive
stories.summary: Florida bonneted bats rebounded from near-extinction to over 200 individuals in specially designed homes, growing from zero in 2018 to the world's largest known population.

stories.headline: First Medical X-Rays Taken in Space Open New Frontier
stories.summary: Astronauts aboard SpaceX's Fram2 took the first diagnostic-quality X-rays in orbit after more than 65 years of human spaceflight, transforming space medicine possibilities.

stories.headline: Against All Odds, West African Leopards Make Their Comeback
stories.summary: In Benin's Pendjari National Park, leopard population tripled in six years despite regional conflict, defying extinction odds.

stories.headline: Microbes Clean Toxic Mine Water: 96% of Uranium Removed in Months
stories.summary: Native bacteria at a flooded German uranium mine transformed toxic dissolved uranium into solid form, proving nature's power to heal.
